response: <<the sprinter's velocity function is given by v(t) = at^3 - bt^2 + ct + d>> <<the maximum velocity occurs at t = 3 seconds, so v(3) = 12 m/s>> <<the derivative of v(t) is dv/dt = 3at^2 - 2bt + c>> <<the maximum velocity is achieved when the derivative is zero, so 3at^2 - 2bt + c = 0>> <<the sprinter's maximum velocity is 12 m/s, so 3a(3)^2 - 2b(3) + c = 12>> <<the equation becomes 27a - 6b + c = 12>> <<the relationship between a, b, and c is 27a - 6b + c = 12>> <<the average speed is the integral of v(t) from 0 to 5 divided by 5>> <<the integral of v(t) from 0 to 5 is ∫(at^3 - bt^2 + ct + d) dt from 0 to 5>> <<the integral evaluates to [ (a/4)t^4 - (b/3)t^3 + (c/2)t^2 + dt ] from 0 to 5>> <<the average speed is [ (a/4)(5)^4 - (b/3)(5)^3 + (c/2)(5)^2 + d(5) ] / 5>> <<the average speed is [ (a/4)(625) - (b/3)(125) + (c/2)(25) + 5d ] / 5>> <<the expression simplifies to (625a/4 - 125b/3 + 25c/2 + 5d)/5>> <<the average speed is (125a/4 - 25b/3 + 5c/2 + d) m/s>> <<the letter w appears in the expression 125a/4 - 25b/3 + 5c/2 + d>> <<the letter w appears in the expression 125a/4 - 25b/3 + 5c/2 + d>> <<the letter w appears in the expression 125a/4 - 2
